question_title: Will the interest in “univision” change between 2026-04-01 and 2026-04-08 according to Google Trends?
summary: The April 1 value of 100 is the PEAK of the entire resolution window (March 9 - April 8), driven by the Supreme Court birthright citizenship oral arguments (Trump v. Barbara). This creates extreme asymmetry: for the value to NOT decrease by April 8, search volume would need to remain at 97%+ of the peak day — historically unprecedented for post-spike dynamics. Previous spikes in Univision interest (e.g., Feb 20 spike of 78) decayed to 55% of peak within 7 days. The SCOTUS hearing was a single-day event with no comparable follow-up expected on April 8. While Mexico vs Belgium on April 5 may create a mid-week bump, it won't approach the SCOTUS hearing magnitude and will fade by April 8. Decreases is overwhelmingly likely.

factors.description: April 1 value is 100 (the PEAK of the entire resolution window). For April 8 to not decrease, it would need search volume at least 97% of the peak day — an extreme requirement given Google Trends scaling.

The April 1 spike was driven by SCOTUS birthright citizenship oral arguments — a single-day event. Historical spike decay shows rapid return to baseline: Feb 20 spike (78) dropped to 43 seven days later (55% retention). Expected April 8 value is ~50-60 in resolution window.
From elevated starting points (>=60), ALL observed 7-day forward changes in the resolution window data show decreases. Zero instances of sustained or increasing values from elevated starts.
Mexico vs Belgium friendly on April 5 could generate a mid-week bump, but soccer events haven't matched SCOTUS hearing magnitude. The bump would likely fade by April 8 (Wednesday).
factors.conditional: If Mexico vs Belgium generates significant interest

factors.description: Scheduled Univision programming on April 8 (Champions League or similar at 15:00) could create some uplift but routine programming doesn't generate peak-level interest.
An unforeseen major news event affecting the Hispanic community on April 8 could match or exceed April 1 interest. Low base rate probability for any specific day.

reasoning:
**Forecast: Univision Google Trends Change (Apr 1-8, 2026)**

| Outcome | Probability |
|---------|-------------|
| **Decreases** | **91%** |
| Increases | 5% |
| Doesn't change | 4% |

**Key reasoning:**

- **April 1 = 100 (the peak)**: The starting value is the maximum in the entire resolution window, driven by the Supreme Court birthright citizenship hearing (Trump v. Barbara oral arguments). This creates extreme asymmetry — there's almost nowhere to go but down.
- **Post-spike decay is rapid and consistent**: The Feb 20 spike (78) decayed to 43 within 7 days (55% retention). All observed 7-day changes from elevated starts showed decreases.
- **No comparable event expected on April 8**: The SCOTUS decision won't come for weeks. Mexico vs Belgium (Apr 5) may create a brief bump but won't match SCOTUS magnitude. The expected April 8 value is roughly 50-60 in the resolution window — a decrease of 40-50 points, far exceeding the ±3 threshold.
- **For "Doesn't change"**: April 8 would need 97%+ of April 1's absolute search volume — effectively requiring another event of comparable magnitude on that exact day.
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) Will the interest in “univision” change between 2026-04-01 and 2026-04-08 according to Google Trends?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/43010)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves “Doesn't change” if the value on the timeline at [https://trends.google.com/trends/explore?geo=US&tz=0&q=univision&date=2026-03-09%202026-04-08](https://trends.google.com/trends/explore?geo=US&tz=0&q=univision&date=2026-03-09%202026-04-08) for 2026-04-08 is within 3 of the value at 2026-04-01. It resolves “Increases” if the value is more than 3 greater, and resolves “Decreases” if the value is more than 3 lower.
fine_print:
A script will be used to determine the resolution of this question. It will access the data on Google Trends using [SerpApi](https://serpapi.com/), and compare the height of the timeline for the two aforementioned dates. The specific python query will be structured as follows:
```
from serpapi import GoogleSearch

search = GoogleSearch(params={
  "api_key": API_KEY,
  "engine": "google_trends",
  "data_type": "TIMESERIES",
  "geo": "US",
  "tz": 0,
  "q": "univision",
  "date": "2026-03-09 2026-04-08",
})
search.get_dict()
```
Note that there may be differences between the results returned by the API and the data appearing on the page. This seems to be due to the 'tz' parameter not having the intended effect in-browser. In this case, the API results will be considered authoritative.
Note that the precision of the timeline will be 1 day, so this will compare the overall interest for the whole day as determined by Google Trends.
Dates are determined in UTC.
If the data is no longer available, or the script fails, this question will be annulled or manually resolved by a moderator.
